auth_notes: Authentication typically involves SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Most OData services require a two-step process for write operations: a GET request to fetch an 'x-csrf-token' followed by the actual request using that token and the session cookies.
rate_limits: Varies by tenant configuration and SAP BTP service plan; typically governed by concurrent request limits and API Management quotas.
gotchas: CSRF token handling is mandatory for POST/PATCH/DELETE and requires cookie persistence between nodes.
OData protocol nuances (v2 vs v4) affect how filters and expansions are structured in the query parameters.
On-premise deployments require the SAP Cloud Connector to expose APIs to the internet/n8n.
client_must_provide: SAP BTP Service Key or OAuth 2.0 Client ID/Secret
Specific OData Service technical names (e.g., API_SALES_ORDER_SRV)
research_notes: Development will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. To handle CSRF tokens, use a 'Wait' or 'Execute Workflow' pattern to ensure cookies from the token-fetch GET request are passed into the subsequent mutation request. Use the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to test OData queries ($filter, $top, $expand) before implementing in n8n.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Development will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. To handle CSRF tokens, use a 'Wait' or 'Execute Workflow' pattern to ensure cookies from the token-fetch GET request are passed into the subsequent mutation request.
